Text.value: R200, 100 .. 550 mm, active high, configurable, Q2 output, cable 2 m, 4-pole
R200, 100 .. 550 mm, active high, configurable, M8 plug, 3-pin
R200, 100 .. 550 mm, active high, configurable, Q2 output, M12 plug, 4-pin
R200, 100 .. 550 mm, active high, configurable, Q2 output, M8 plug, 4-pin
R200, 100 .. 550 mm, active high, configurable, M8 pigtail, 3-pin
R200, 100 .. 550 mm, active high, configurable, Q2 output, M8 pigtail, 4-pin
R200, 100 .. 550 mm, active high, configurable, Q2 output, M12 pigtail, 4-pin
current status of the switching signal 1
current status of the switching signal 2
quality of the evaluated sensor signal, quality drops with weak signal or interference
value for the setpoint acquired via a teach-in process
provides additional tagging posibilities e.g. for a application or job configuration
provides additional tagging posibilities for location of the device
shows the current state and progress of a teach-in process
shows the current state and progress of a teach-in process
setpoint values for switching signal channel 1
setpoint values for switching signal channel 2
setpoint 1 value for switching signal channel
setpoint 2 value for switching signal channel
configuration parameter for switching signal channel 1
configuration parameter for switching signal channel 2
defines the logical behaviour of the switching signal and derived output signal
defines the evaluation behaviour of the switching signal and derived output signal
a higher hysteresis may help to increase stability in critical applications
configuration of switching delay functions and times for switching channel 1
configuration of switching delay functions and times for switching channel 1
defines the switching delay for a on-to-off transition or the minimum on time after a off-to-on transition of the switching signal depending on the delay mode setting'
defines switching delay for a off-to-on transition of the switching signal
selects between a combination of 'on' and 'off' delay times or 'on' and 'one-shot' delay times
the setpoint offset defines the switchpoint of the sensor relative to the setpoint
the setpoint offset defines the switchpoint of the sensor relative to the setpoint
configuration of filtering may help solve critical applications
offset for calibration of zero reference for a certain mounting situation
configures the output or input characteristic at pin 2 or white wire
configuration of the specific function at the additional I/O terminal if configured as output
configuration of the specific function at the additional I/O terminal if configured as input
allows enabling or disabling specific functions controlled by PD Output
enabled: the emitter is controlled via process data output - a '1' at 'PD Output - Tansducer disable' disables the emitter, object detection is inhibited
enabled: the signal evaluation is controlled via process data output - a '1' at 'PDoutput - Evaluation Hold' stops signal evaluation, the last state will be maintained
different event sources can be en- or disabled
enabled: an event is generated if operating reserve is below configured threshold at parameter 'Operating Reserve'
enabled: an event is generated if sensor signals do not allow a valid evaluation
enabled: an event is generated if sensor signals are not valid due to e.g. heavy interferences
enabled: an event is generated if the emitter is set to test mode (turned off, transducer disable) by a remote signal
allows setting the device into different operational states e.g. for diagnosis purposes
enables temporary adjustment or teach-in over local control elements
enables a defined flashing pattern of the indicator LEDs for better spotting a sensor in field application
shows the overall hours of operation
indicates operation at critical ambient temperatures or excess of specification limits
the minimum value of the specified detection range
the maximum value of the specified detection range
the maximum specified supply current excluding load
true: emitter is disabled, object detection is inhibited
true: local controls are temporarily enabled for adjustment or teach-in in IO-Link mode
descriptor for interpretation of the measurement data channel (process data MV)
descriptor for the available features at the electrical interface
Analog output on pin 2 / white wire
Second signal in- / output on pin 2 / white wire
Second signal in- / output / analog output on pin 2 / white wire
Analog output on pin 2/wh, input signal on pin 5 / grey wire
Second signal output on pin 2 / white, input signal on pin 5 / grey wire
Second signal / analog output on pin 2 / white, input signal on pin 5 / grey wire
Invalid combination of setpoint and measurement offset values
measurement offset and setpoint setting result in a switchpoint outside of the specified detection range, check values
Stability alarm - operating reserve too low
Check sensor adjustment or clean surface
Check sensor adjustment or target position
Check for excessive ambient light interference
Ambient temperature outside specified temperature range
Device set to test mode (emitter off) by remote signal
Teach-in of setpoint 1 has been executed
Teach-in of setpoint 2 has been executed
Teach-in of measurement offset has been executed
Parameters are set to factory settings
